messages.content: You are a citation classifier. Determine the authenticity of references and their individual components.

Volk RJ, Lewis KB, Smith M, Carley M, Barry MJ, Bekker HL, Härter M, Hoffmann T, McCaffery K, Pignone M, Steffensen KD, Sepucha K, Thompson R, Trevena L, van der Weijden T, Witteman HO, Stacey D. Updated International Patient Decision Aid Standards (IPDAS version 5.0): modified Delphi, evidence informed consensus process. BMJ. 2026;393:. doi:10.1136/bmj-2025-088116

reasoning.description: 1 to 2 sentences outlining the reasoning behind your classification.

authors.reasoning: The authors listed are prominent members of the IPDAS Collaboration and have published previous versions together.

title.reasoning: The title follows the naming convention of previous updates, but there is no record of an 'IPDAS 5.0' being published in the BMJ as of early 2024.

journal_name.reasoning: BMJ is a legitimate and highly reputable medical journal often associated with these standards.

journal_volume.reasoning: The BMJ current volume is around 384 in 2024; volume 393 corresponds to a future date around 2026 and is currently non-existent.

journal_issue.reasoning: No issue number was provided in the citation, though BMJ often uses elocators instead of traditional issues now.

journal_page.reasoning: No page numbers were provided in the reference string.

elocator.reasoning: The elocator is missing from the citation text.

publication_date.reasoning: The year 2026 is in the future, meaning this specific article has not been published yet.

doi.reasoning: The DOI 10.1136/bmj-2025-088116 contains a year prefix (2025) that does not match any current active BMJ registrations for this topic.

pmid.reasoning: No PMID was provided in the citation.

pmcid.reasoning: No PMCID was provided in the citation.

REFERENCE.reasoning: While based on real authors and a real topic, the specific publication record (2026, Vol 393, and DOI) is a hallucination of a future event.
